subject: english
chapter_title: The World of Limitless Possibilities (The World of Limitless Possibilities)

main_sections.title: The World of Limitless Possibilities
main_sections.prose_summary: This informational text introduces the Paralympic Games as a global platform that celebrates resilience and extraordinary talent of athletes with locomotor, sensory, or intellectual disabilities. Bharat's Paralympic journey dates to 1968, with the first medal won in swimming in 1972. The text centres on a detailed interview with Dr. Deepa Malik — Khel Ratna, Arjuna, and Padma Shri awardee — one of the 10 most inspirational women para-athletes globally as recognised by the International Paralympic Committee. At 29, Dr. Malik was diagnosed with a spinal tumour; post-surgery she was declared permanently paralysed waist down and wheelchair-bound. Faced with two choices — squandering her life in remorse or transforming it into a world of limitless possibilities — she chose the latter. Drawing on her pre-existing love of swimming, she transitioned to para-athletics. Her breakthrough came at the 2016 Rio Paralympic Games where she won silver in the shot-put event. She holds multiple 'firsts': first Indian female para-athlete to win an Asian Games medal in athletics, and India's first female Paralympic medallist across any sport. She articulates that physical limitations do not define potential and champions the philosophy of 'ability beyond disability'. Challenges she overcame included societal underestimation, which she met with mental resilience, family support, and fortitude. She views Paralympics as a force that breaks preconceived notions about disability. Beyond sport, Dr. Malik advocates for disability rights: she strengthens the emotional health of persons with challenges, empowers women through outdoor sports, provides equipment to para sportspersons, and organises disability-sports awareness sessions in schools and colleges. Her closing advice — every setback is an opportunity to prove strength; disability is not a limitation but a unique strength waiting to be unleashed — encapsulates the chapter's central theme.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Reflect and Respond + Reading for Meaning + Check Your Understanding + Critical Reflection

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Read the two sets of sentences given below. Choose whether the given statements display: fact-opinion or cause-effect.
(1)(i) In the 2016 Rio Paralympic Games, I secured the silver medal in the shot-put event. (ii) I feel it was a moment of personal victory and a step forward in changing perceptions.
(2)(i) I was diagnosed with a tumour in my spine. (ii) I had a surgery and was told that I would be bound to a wheelchair for the rest of my life.

question_bank.stem: Identify whether set (1) shows fact-opinion or cause-effect.
Identify whether set (2) shows fact-opinion or cause-effect.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Work in pairs to identify which of these pairs of sentences show: 1. cause-effect  2. fact-opinion. Write 1 or 2 in the boxes given.
(i) Dr. Deepa Malik was a member of the Working Group for the Formulation of Twelfth Five Year Plan (2012–17). / It is believed that she contributed greatly to create better sports policies for the persons with disabilities.
(ii) Paralympics showcases incredible talent and determination of para-athletes. / The global perception on disabilities shifts, inspiring para-athletes to pursue their dreams.

question_bank.stem: Identify pair (i): cause-effect or fact-opinion?
Identify pair (ii): cause-effect or fact-opinion?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Read the extracts given below and answer the questions that follow.
Extract 1: 'I had two choices—squander my life in remorse or transform it to a world of limitless possibilities. I love sports and had been a swimmer too, so I decided to switch to para-athletics. This is how my Paralympics journey began. My breakthrough moment came in the 2016 Rio Paralympic Games, when I secured the silver medal in the shot-put event. In hindsight, I feel it was a moment of personal victory and a step forward in changing perceptions.'

question_bank.stem: Give a reason for the following statement: The speaker's decision to transform her life can be likened to the concept of a caterpillar transforming into a butterfly.
Why could the speaker switch to para-athletics quite comfortably?
Complete the sentence with an appropriate reason. The speaker calls 2016 Rio Paralympic Games as a 'breakthrough moment' because ________.
Select the correct option to complete the sentence. The phrase 'in hindsight' indicates the speaker is __________.
The speaker uses the phrase 'changing perceptions'. List one likely perception the speaker might have changed.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read Extract 2 and answer the questions: 'Honestly, I feel sports, especially Paralympics, have the extraordinary ability to challenge stereotypes and change attitudes towards disability. When people witness the strength, skill, and competitive spirit of para-athletes, it breaks down preconceived notions. Paralympics has given me a new lease of life and helped me push boundaries.'

question_bank.stem: Choose the option that lists the words to describe the tone of the speaker: A. appreciative, B. moralistic, C. sentimental, D. optimistic, E. defensive. Options: (a) A and D  (b) B, D, and E  (c) B and C  (d) A, C, and E
Complete the analogy given below with a word from the extract. ability : potential :: preconceived notions: __________
State whether the following sentence is true or false. Paralympics is a platform that not only showcases the remarkable abilities of para-athletes but also questions stereotypes.
What does 'helped me push boundaries' tell us about the speaker?
Which phrase from the extract suggests that the speaker was able to transform her life?
Explain how Dr. Malik's achievements challenge societal perceptions.
What can be the long-term impact of involving youth in schools and colleges in advocacy with respect to disability?
Rationalise the appropriateness of the title of this text with reference to Dr. Deepa Malik's attitude and achievements.
'Every setback is an opportunity to prove your strength'. How might this be a life lesson for every individual, and not just sportspersons?
Examine how Dr. Deepa Malik's recognition as one of the 10 most inspirational para-athletes globally contributes to the larger discourse on gender equality in sports.
How might Dr. Deepa Malik's thought, 'ability beyond disability', serve as a guideline for success for all future para-athletes?
What have you learnt from this interview, and how can you implement these learnings in your life?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Work in pairs. Match the given phrases in Column 1 with their meanings in Column 2. Use the phrases in sentences of your own.

question_bank.stem: Match: 1. defy the odds  2. a new lease of life  3. in hindsight  4. breakthrough moment  5. turn obstacles into stepping stones — with (i)–(v).
question_bank.table:
Phrase|Meaning
defy the odds|(iii) succeed in doing something when most people think it would result in failure
a new lease of life|(v) an occasion when you become more energetic and active than earlier
in hindsight|(iv) ability to understand something after it has happened
breakthrough moment|(i) a time of significant development or discovery
turn obstacles into stepping stones|(ii) overcome challenges and achieve goals

tasks_verbatim.task_text: A common word used in athletics is 'sprinting' which relates to movement. Arrange the words related to movement on the word cline in increasing order of pace from the slowest to the fastest: crawl, amble, walk, jog, saunter, dart, sprint, stroll, run, plod, creep.

question_bank.stem: Arrange words on pace cline (slowest to fastest): crawl, creep, plod, amble, saunter, stroll, walk, jog, run, dart, sprint.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Match the highlighted verbs in Column 1 with their functions in Column 2. There are two extra functions that you do not need.
1. I could hear the audience clapping.  2. It couldn't be helped.  3. I should give it my best shot.  4. I knew if I tried, I would win.

question_bank.stem: Match modal verbs to functions: could → past ability; couldn't → impossibility; should → obligation; would → prediction.
question_bank.table:
Sentence|Function
I could hear the audience clapping|(v) past ability
It couldn't be helped|(vi) impossibility
I should give it my best shot|(iv) obligation
I knew if I tried, I would win|(iii) prediction

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the table which shows other functions of the modal auxiliaries: could (possibility, unreal ability, request, suggestion), couldn't (inability), should (probability, advice), would (request, past habit). Now, make sentences with the modal verbs given in the table above with each function. Mention the function in brackets.

question_bank.stem: Write one sentence each for: could (possibility), could (unreal ability), could (request), could (suggestion), couldn't (inability), should (probability), should (advice), would (request), would (past habit).

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentences. The sentences given above are in ______________________. (Direct Speech/Reported Speech). The sentences given above are ___________ sentences. (declarative/interrogative/exclamatory/imperative). Now, work in pairs. Observe the changes from Column 1 to Column 2. Note these changes in Column 3. One example has been done for you.

question_bank.stem: Identify speech type: Direct Speech or Reported Speech?
Identify sentence type: declarative/interrogative/exclamatory/imperative?
Note changes in Column 3 for: Dr. Malik: I love sports and had been a swimmer too... → Dr. Malik said that she loved sports...
Note changes in Column 3 for: Interviewer: Your story is indeed a testament... → The interviewer said that her story was indeed a testament...
Note changes in Column 3 for: Dr. Malik: I believe youth is the voice of tomorrow. → Dr. Malik says that she believes youth is the voice of tomorrow.

question_bank.stem: Convert the Siya–Tarun conversation (4 blanks) to reported speech.

listening.transcript_text: Good morning everyone. We are excited to announce a special assembly on the occasion of World Inclusion Day. This assembly will take place on Thursday, 10 October, at 9.00 a.m. in the school auditorium. We have a series of engaging programmes planned for the day to celebrate and promote inclusion. The assembly will commence with a welcome speech by our Principal, followed by a staging on inclusion by the Interact Club. There will also be a special performance by the school's dance team that highlights themes of diversity and acceptance. After the performances, we will have a panel discussion featuring guest speakers who are advocates for inclusion in various fields. We will conclude with a song presented by students to nurture inclusive practices and collaboration among all members of our school community. Thank you.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to an announcement for special assembly in school. As you listen, fill in the blanks with one or two exact words. (Transcript for teacher on page 268)

question_bank.stem: 1. The venue for the special assembly is the ______________.
2. A presentation on inclusion will be made by the ________________.
3. The dance performance will focus on the themes of ___________ and ____________.
4. A _______________ will be conducted by experts who support inclusion.
5. The celebration will end with a _______________ to encourage team work.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Interview the Sports Captain and the Sports Coach of your school. Note that the interview of the Sports Captain is informal, and that of the Sports Coach is formal. Work in pairs. One of you can be the interviewer and the other the interviewee in the informal interview. Interchange your role for the formal one.

question_bank.stem: Conduct informal interview of Sports Captain using prompts: Tell me about yourself and your love for sports. / Did you face any challenges? How did you deal with them? / What are some impressive achievements you've had? / What would you like to say to your peers or juniors?

Conduct formal interview of Sports Coach using prompts: Could you tell me how your journey in sports began? / Could you share something about the challenges you faced and how you overcame them? / You've received many accolades. What do these accomplishments mean to you? / What advice do you have for aspiring athletes?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the details about a notice in the table given below. The Sports Club of your school is organising an Inter-school Athletic Meet. As the Sports Captain, draft a notice in not more than 50 words asking interested students to register themselves for selection in different events. Add all necessary details. Do not forget to design your notice within a box.

question_bank.stem: Draft a notice (max 50 words, within a box) for an Inter-school Athletic Meet inviting student registrations. Include: Name of Organisation, NOTICE, Date, Heading, Details, Signature, Name, Designation.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Did you know? (Reading about the origin of Paralympics: Sir Ludwig Guttmann, 1948, Stoke Mandeville Games, Rome 1960, Winter and Summer Games alternately every two years, held in the same city as the Olympics since the late twentieth century.)

Work in groups of four. Choose any one of the topics given below and make a presentation. Topics: (1) Two lesser-known Paralympic sports (examples: para equestrian, para rowing, etc.) (2) Two Paralympians from India. Steps to be followed: Do research referring to magazines, newspapers, and the internet. Prepare presentation: one introductory slide, two slides on origin/history of Paralympics, four to five slides about the chosen topic, one concluding slide. Choose appropriate slide design, colour schemes, and images. Ensure good balance between text and images. Share and present in class (each member takes turns).

question_bank.stem: Choose one topic and prepare a group presentation: (a) Two lesser-known Paralympic sports OR (b) Two Paralympians from India.
